div.h1: Bathroom Renovation & Design Specialists in Dublin
div.p: Craik & Comfort is positioned around a complete renovation journey for Dublin homeowners: design guidance, product supply, project organisation, and a clearer route from first consultation to final bathroom finish.

div.h2: What the renovation service covers.
div.p: This page is structured to target homeowners searching for bathroom renovation support in Dublin, while clearly showing what Craik & Comfort manages during the project.
Define the room goals, the practical constraints, and the level of finish needed before products or quotes are finalised.
Bring clarity to spacing, product compatibility, storage needs, and the visual direction of the new bathroom.
Align basins, baths, brassware, enclosures, and related products with the real installation plan.
Keep the finishing stage cleaner by reviewing the selected products, project details, and sign-off priorities.

div.h2: Designed for homeowners who want a clearer renovation plan.
div.p: Irish renovation enquiries tend to move faster when the website explains the process in plain language. This page now frames Craik & Comfort as a project-focused partner, not only a product source.

ul.li: Room goals, measurements, and layout direction are discussed before product decisions are locked in.
Product selection is matched to installation logic, not treated as a disconnected shop order.
Installer communication can be supported where the client already has a plumber or fitter.
The service message stays anchored around Design, Supply, and Finalise.

div.h2: What clients usually ask first.
div.p: The page answers the core commercial questions without overpromising exact numbers before scope has been reviewed.

div.span: Consultation, product lead times, and installer coordination are explained early.
Finish level, room size, and product priorities are reviewed before the quote is finalised.
The page language is aimed at renovation searches in Dublin and the wider Irish market.
Dublin, Dublin 15, surrounding areas, and selected Ireland-wide supply enquiries.

div.h2: Typical renovation briefs the site can convert.
div.p: Instead of generic brochure copy, the page can now guide visitors by the type of bathroom project they are trying to complete.
Update dated layouts, replace worn products, and coordinate a room that feels cleaner, brighter, and easier to use daily.
Improve compact spaces with stronger planning, more suitable showering solutions, and a tighter finish selection.
Bring together a statement bath, refined fittings, and more polished specification support for higher-end projects.

div.h2: Ready to plan your bathroom renovation in Dublin?
div.p: Move the visitor toward a quote or consultation instead of leaving them at the browsing stage.

div.h1: Bathroom Design Service for Dublin Renovation Projects
div.p: This page supports homeowners who need help planning the room before final product selection. It gives Craik & Comfort a dedicated landing page for design consultation, layout planning, and style direction.

div.h2: What the design service helps with.
div.p: Design-led visitors often need a clearer route to room planning before they are ready to buy. This page is built around that earlier-stage demand.
Review how the room should flow, what fits practically, and which products make sense together.
Match white ceramics, warm metals, glass systems, and stone-inspired tones with the right level of contrast.
Choose the basins, baths, shower solutions, and fittings that support the project scope without confusion.
Reduce guesswork by aligning the design intent with product decisions before installation begins.

div.h2: Design support that feels practical, not vague.
div.p: The page positions Craik & Comfort as a bathroom design guide for Dublin clients who want help translating inspiration into a room that can actually be delivered.

ul.li: Plan layouts before the wrong products are ordered.
Coordinate finishes so the room feels consistent, not pieced together.
Use product guidance to shorten the decision stage.
Keep the project message connected to the later supply and finalise stages.

div.h2: Styles this page can support.
div.p: These style cues give the website stronger relevance for design-led searches and consultation enquiries.

div.h2: Why this page matters commercially.
div.p: Not every visitor is ready to buy immediately. A dedicated design service page captures earlier-stage leads that still convert into product supply and renovation work later.
Clients are less likely to order items that do not suit the room size, layout, or installer requirements.
Visitors reach out with more context once the design process is clearly explained on the site.
Design consultation naturally leads into the supply and project organisation conversation.

div.h1: Bathroom Supply & Shop for Dublin and Ireland
div.p: This page bridges the commercial gap between service-led renovation enquiries and the actual product catalog. It gives the shop a stronger role inside the renovation journey.

div.h2: Core product groups customers buy during renovations.
div.p: The page now aligns the shop with real renovation buying behaviour instead of leaving products disconnected from the service story.

a.p: Freestanding and built-in bathing options suited to premium room upgrades and full bathroom reworks.
Bath screens and glass-led systems that support practical layouts without closing the room in visually.
Vanity-led focal pieces and supporting basin selections for more resolved bathroom compositions.
Brassware, fittings, and finishing details that tie the whole room together.

div.h2: Supply that supports the project, not just the checkout.
div.p: For renovation clients, the shop works best when it is backed by delivery guidance, product coordination, and communication that fits a live project.

ul.li: Shop products directly from the website.
Ask for help matching products across the same room scheme.
Coordinate supply with installers and project timing.
Support enquiries in Dublin and wider Ireland where relevant.

div.h2: What this page needs to communicate.
div.p: Irish homeowners want clarity around availability, support, and whether the supplier understands a renovation workflow.

div.span: Give visitors a direct route into the product catalog.
Position the business for Dublin delivery and selected Ireland-wide enquiries.
Show that product supply can be aligned with renovation planning.
Encourage product advice requests before a larger order is confirmed.

div.h1: Besco Bathroom Products for Dublin and Irish Renovation Projects
div.p: This page gives Craik & Comfort a dedicated Besco landing page for search visibility, product discovery, and design-led renovation enquiries.

div.h2: Besco collections that support a stronger room story.
div.p: The page is structured around the product families homeowners actually compare when planning a renovation.
Compact and statement bathing options for family bathrooms, ensuites, and premium main-room upgrades.
Trays, enclosures, and related components for clean layouts with stronger practical performance.
Use Besco imagery and product groups to guide clients toward layouts that feel calm, modern, and consistent.
Direct catalog downloads help clients, installers, and project decision-makers review collections quickly.

div.h2: Catalogs and product resources.
div.p: These assets turn the Besco page into a stronger reference point for homeowners and renovation decision-makers.
A shower enclosure catalog covering doors, cabins, and system options relevant to modern bathroom projects.
Useful for ceramic-led bathroom planning and broader basin selections that support design consultations.
A compact product overview for faster sharing during consultation, supply, and project coordination conversations.

div.h1: Bathroom Project Organisation for Clients Who Already Have an Installer
div.p: Many renovation clients already know a plumber or fitter but still need help planning the room, selecting products, and keeping the project aligned. This service page is built around that exact need.

div.h2: How the organisation service differentiates Craik & Comfort.
div.p: This is not a standard supplier-only message. The page gives the brand a more valuable position between the client, the products, and the installer.
Help clients build a room with compatible products rather than separate purchases that create installation issues later.
Sequence the product decisions around the real project flow so ordering, delivery, and fitting stay more coordinated.
Support clearer discussion with the plumber or fitter once the room direction and product set are defined.
Keep the end of the project cleaner by checking the agreed products, finish direction, and remaining decisions.

div.h2: Design, Supply, Finalise.
div.p: This phrase can now be used as a simple commercial explanation of the full service model on the website.

ul.li: Design: define the room intent, layout, finish level, and product direction.
Supply: align the product list with availability, delivery, and project requirements.
Finalise: support the last stage so the room is completed with fewer loose ends.

div.h2: Who this page is for.

div.h1: Before & After Bathroom Projects Built for Trust and Enquiries
div.p: This page is now positioned as the place to publish approved project stories with before photos, after photos, materials used, timeline notes, and budget guidance once the client assets are ready.

div.h2: What each project story should show.
div.p: Irish homeowners tend to trust renovation businesses more when the project page includes the details that matter commercially, not just polished final photography.
Show the starting point so visitors can understand the real change delivered by the renovation plan.
Display the finished room clearly, with materials, fittings, and the overall atmosphere visible.
Include the practical details Irish homeowners compare before requesting a consultation.

div.h2: Project story format ready for approved case studies.
div.p: Until final project assets are supplied, this page is structured to show exactly how renovation stories can be presented.
Ready to display room constraints, selected products, timeline notes, and the final bathroom outcome once approved project content is available.
Designed to showcase the role of layout planning, premium fittings, and room-wide product coordination.
Structured to present the project brief, key materials, lead-time notes, and the commercial detail that improves trust.

div.h1: Customer Reviews and Trust Signals for Bathroom Renovation Enquiries
div.p: This page gives Craik & Comfort a dedicated trust layer for Google-driven Irish visitors who want social proof before starting a renovation conversation.

div.h2: Review sources this page is ready to support.
div.p: Real approved feedback can be published here from the channels Irish homeowners check most before choosing a renovation partner.
Use this area for approved Google review excerpts and source links that strengthen local trust and search credibility.
Show social proof from Facebook so visitors see consistent trust signals beyond the website itself.
Publish approved project comments tied to renovation scope, product support, or design guidance.

div.h2: Why this page matters in the Irish market.
div.p: For renovation services, trust content is not optional. Visitors comparing providers want visible proof that the business understands projects, communicates clearly, and follows through.

ul.li: Google search traffic converts better when trust content is easy to find.
Project-led reviews support both the service pages and the product pages.
Local market knowledge feels stronger when testimonials reference real project experiences.

div.h2: Trust signals already built into the site.

div.span: Phone, WhatsApp, and email remain prominent across the site.
Renovation, design, and project organisation pages reinforce the core offer.
Request a Quote and consultation CTAs give visitors a direct next step.
Before & After content is now part of the conversion path.

div.h1: Request a Bathroom Renovation or Supply Quote
div.p: This page gives Craik & Comfort a dedicated quote route for renovation, design, product supply, and project organisation enquiries.

div.h2: What to include in your quote request.
div.p: A stronger enquiry usually includes enough context to move the conversation forward quickly.

ul.li: Tell us whether the enquiry is for renovation, design support, supply, or project coordination.
Include the project location, room type, and the stage you are currently at.
Share the products, finishes, or Besco ranges you are considering if known.
Use the message field for timeline notes, budget guidance needs, and installer context.

div.h2: Send your quote request.
div.p: The form is positioned to capture the kind of enquiry that can convert into a consultation or project brief.

textarea.name: body_html
textarea.placeholder: Tell us about the room, location, timeline, products, and the type of help you need.

form.p: Your enquiry routes directly to info@craikcomfort.ie.
